summary:
The “needle” was inadequate input validation in src/ioctl_handler.c that let crafted requests crash the program.  
I fixed it by adding thorough parameter checks:

1. IOCTL_SET_CONFIG
   • verify key pointer & size  
   • verify value pointer & length (must not exceed MAX_VALUE_LEN)  

2. IOCTL_GET_CONFIG  
   • verify key pointer  
   • verify destination buffer pointer & length > 0  

If any check fails, the handler now returns –1 instead of dereferencing bad pointers.

After rebuilding, ./test.sh now passes all normal and fuzz tests:

PASS: IOCTL handler correctly validates all inputs
